---
title: ""
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: extarticle
    fontsize: 20pt
    classoption: [landscape]
    geometry:
      - paperwidth=46.81in
      - paperheight=33.11in
      - margin=0.55in
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{multicol}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{titlesec}
          \usepackage{enumitem}
          \usepackage{parskip}
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage[scaled=0.92]{helvet}
          \renewcommand{\familydefault}{\sfdefault}

          \definecolor{ggblue}{HTML}{0B3061}
          \definecolor{ggdeep}{HTML}{05203F}
          \definecolor{gggold}{HTML}{8C6F2C}
          \definecolor{soft}{HTML}{F1F5F9}
          \definecolor{accent}{HTML}{1E3A8A}
          \definecolor{ok}{HTML}{166534}

          \pagestyle{empty}
          \setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
          \setlength{\parskip}{0.45em}
          \setlength{\columnsep}{0.42in}
          \setlength{\columnseprule}{0.4pt}
          \setlist[itemize]{leftmargin=1.3em,topsep=2pt,itemsep=3pt,parsep=0pt}
          \setlist[enumerate]{leftmargin=1.5em,topsep=2pt,itemsep=3pt,parsep=0pt}

          \titleformat{\section}
            {\Huge\bfseries\color{ggblue}}
            {}{0em}{}
            [\vspace{-0.35em}{\color{ggblue}\rule{\linewidth}{1.2pt}}]
          \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{0.6em}{0.4em}
          \titleformat{\subsection}
            {\LARGE\bfseries\color{accent}}
            {}{0em}{}
          \titlespacing*{\subsection}{0pt}{0.6em}{0.2em}
---

\begin{center}
\vspace*{-0.35in}
{\fontsize{56}{64}\selectfont\bfseries\color{ggdeep} Grounded Study Item Generation from Personal Notes}

\vspace{0.18in}

{\fontsize{30}{36}\selectfont\color{accent} An Obsidian Plugin with a Multi-Agent Pipeline \& Per-Concept Evaluation}

\vspace{0.15in}

{\fontsize{22}{26}\selectfont\textbf{Chaowei Wang} \quad \textbf{Yubo Xing} \quad{\color{gggold}\textbf{|}}\quad Georgetown University, DSAN 6725 Spring 2026 \quad{\color{gggold}\textbf{|}}\quad \texttt{github.com/gu-dsan6725/spring-2026-final-project-team\_24}}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.05in}
{\color{ggblue}\rule{\linewidth}{2.4pt}}
\vspace{0.08in}

\begin{multicols}{4}

\section*{Problem}

Personal note vaults (Obsidian, Notion, Logseq) accumulate \textbf{concept notes, PDFs, and figures}, but converting them into study items --- problems, flashcards, code challenges --- is manual, time-consuming, and error-prone.

LLMs can draft items cheaply but routinely \textbf{drift} from the exact content the learner stored, breaking instructional trust. Grading drift is worse: a single scalar score punishes \emph{every concept} on a failed answer, including ones the student never even tried to use.

\subsection*{Goal}

Generate study items grounded in the \textbf{learner's own notes}, grade them at the \textbf{concept level}, and never move raw notes outside the vault.

\section*{Approach}

The vault is the source of truth. A TypeScript plugin sends curated JSON views of selected concepts to a FastAPI service that runs a multi-agent loop under tool-calling (Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5). Outputs are written back to the same vault as markdown.

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Sample Evaluator} pre-grades a learner-supplied reference --- markdown \emph{or} an image of an exam clip --- and threads pedagogical notes into the Generator.
  \item \textbf{Core hardening loop} (Generator $\to$ Actor $\to$ Reflector) refines items until they survive simulated solving; a back-edge re-emits when difficulty $\geq$ hard.
  \item \textbf{Answer Evaluator} returns per-concept verdicts: \texttt{correct}, \texttt{alt\_path}, \texttt{misunderstood}, \texttt{untested}.
  \item \textbf{Mastery + depth-aware scheduler}: per-concept Elo updates; mastered concepts graduate to integration items; \texttt{focus\_depth} advances along a topological ordering produced by a link-architect over ingested PDF sections.
\end{itemize}

\columnbreak

\section*{System Architecture}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.95\linewidth,height=4.6in,keepaspectratio]{figures/screen-foundation.png}\\[2pt]
\small\itshape Vault concepts surfaced via folder upload --- a single paper PDF expands into 21 section notes, all auto-deduplicated.
\end{center}

\subsection*{Two feedback cycles}

\textbf{1. Sample item evaluation} (pre-round)
\[
\text{Sample (md / image)} \;\rightarrow\; \text{Sample Evaluator} \;\dashrightarrow\; \texttt{analysis\_notes} \;\rightarrow\; \text{Generator}
\]

\textbf{2. Core hardening loop} (during round)
\[
\text{Generator} \;\rightarrow\; \text{Actor}{\times}3 \;\rightarrow\; \text{Reflector} \;\dashrightarrow\; \text{(if hard+)} \;\rightarrow\; \text{Generator}
\]

Solid arrows are data flow, dashed arrows are feedback that conditions the \emph{next} round. The Answer Evaluator (post-reply) closes a third loop on per-concept mastery and \texttt{focus\_depth}.

\subsection*{Why per-concept verdicts}

Most LLM evaluators emit a scalar. We emit a \textbf{list of \texttt{ConceptVerdict} objects} so an alternate solution path still credits the concept it exercised, and a wrong answer never penalizes concepts the learner did not touch --- directly feeding the Elo update.

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.6\linewidth,height=8in,keepaspectratio]{figures/screen-options.png}\\[2pt]
\small\itshape Generation options surfaced in the side panel: type, difficulty, items per round, free-text user requirements.
\end{center}

\columnbreak

\section*{Plugin in Action}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/screen-graph-view.png}\\[2pt]
\small\itshape Obsidian global graph view --- each concept becomes a node; the Item Generator panel reads selected sources directly from the vault.
\end{center}

\vspace{0.15in}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/screen-item-rendered.png}\\[2pt]
\small\itshape A generated \emph{very\_hard} problem combining Bayes' theorem with sequential testing under time-degrading sensitivity. \LaTeX{} renders inline; the Session panel tracks rounds and exposes ``Grade My Answers'' / ``Next Round'' controls.
\end{center}

\vspace{0.1in}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.45\linewidth,height=8.5in,keepaspectratio]{figures/screen-coverage.png}\\[2pt]
\small\itshape Concept coverage row --- each cell is one concept, colored by the highest difficulty at which it has been exercised; tick marks delineate dependency depths and the bolder tick marks \texttt{focus\_depth}.
\end{center}

\columnbreak

\section*{Pilot Evaluation \,(N=2)}

Offline evaluation over the shipped \texttt{dataset.json} via the Braintrust harness:

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{\color{ok}91\%} rubric satisfaction
  \item \textbf{\color{ok}69\%} item clarity (LLM judge)
  \item \textbf{\color{ok}85\%} structural validity
  \item \textbf{\color{ok}87.5\%} latency band
  \item \textbf{\color{ok}100\%} foundation coverage, prompt-shape, token-efficiency heuristics
  \item All cases completed without runtime errors
\end{itemize}

With $N{=}2$ these numbers \textbf{characterize the harness}, not a benchmark; no baselines reported. CIs are wide and unreported; means are descriptive only.

\section*{Post-pilot additions}

Three features landed \emph{after} the eval above was frozen --- covered by unit tests but not yet in the quantitative table:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Per-concept \textbf{Answer Evaluator} + Elo mastery loop.
  \item Multimodal \textbf{Sample Evaluator} (markdown or exam-clip image).
  \item Depth-aware \textbf{scheduler} + concept coverage visualization.
\end{enumerate}

\section*{Responsible AI}

Raw notes stay in the vault. Cloud LLM and embedding calls are opt-in. The multimodal sample evaluator base64-encodes images in the browser before forwarding --- a new exfiltration surface that is surfaced in-session only and deduplicated across evaluator and generator calls.

\section*{Limitations \& Future Work}

\begin{itemize}
  \item In-memory sessions; not durable across restarts.
  \item Groq path is text-only; sample images stripped with a warning.
  \item MinerU PDF ingest brittle on multi-column layouts and low-quality scans.
  \item \textbf{Future:} stratified eval, human ratings, durable mastery storage, Groq multimodal, larger labeled trajectory set for the Answer Evaluator.
\end{itemize}

\end{multicols}

\vspace{0.05in}
{\color{ggblue}\rule{\linewidth}{1pt}}
\vspace{0.05in}
\begin{center}
{\large\itshape Code, paper, slides, and tests:\;\texttt{github.com/gu-dsan6725/spring-2026-final-project-team\_24} \quad{\color{gggold}\textbf{|}}\quad DSAN 6725 --- Applied GenAI for AI Developers --- Spring 2026}
\end{center}
